shadows.name: glow
shadows.purpose: Soft amber glow for active card components.

shadows.name: elegant
shadows.purpose: Active shadow during interactions and hover states.

components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background-color: #5d4037; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 32px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-weight: 600; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #8b4513; }

components.name: Text Input
components.css: .ds-input { background-color: #fdfaf6; border: 1px solid #ede3d7; border-radius: 8px; padding: 14px 16px; color: #1a0f0a; font-size: 14px; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; transition: border-color 0.25s, box-shadow 0.25s; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #5d4037; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(93, 64, 55, 0.06); }

narrative.overview: Yuno is a geospatial social network designed to evoke the feeling of a warm, tactile field notebook or a physical map spread across a wood-grain table. The interface rejects sterile, generic SaaS-cream layouts and cyber-neon hues in favor of organic textures, leather anchors, and pulsing amber highlights that call attention to real-time human activity.
keyCharacteristics: Earthy, high-contrast color scheme anchored in deep leather browns and warm neutrals.
Tight information density suitable for mobile coordinates and map controls.
Micro-interactions emphasizing live city activity with pulsing orange amber tones.

rules.name: The Rarity of Pulse Rule
rules.body: Pulsing amber orange is exclusively used for live real-time activities. No other button, label, indicator, or border may use this hue.

rules.name: The Ink Contrast Rule
rules.body: Never use light gray or low-contrast text on cream/sand surfaces. Body text must always use Almost Black-Brown (#1a0f0a) or Dark Cocoa (#3e2723) to guarantee WCAG AA contrast.

rules.name: The Fixed Ratio Rule
rules.body: Heading sizes are set in fixed pixel values or standard rem increments, never fluid viewport width values, preventing text overflow and clipping on mobile screens.

dos: Do check contrast using a contrast checker for every text element.
Do wrap descriptions and captions in text-wrap: pretty to avoid lone words on single lines.
Do collapse navigation bars and panels cleanly on mobile viewports.
donts: Don't use side-stripe borders (e.g. border-left thicker than 1px) on alerts or cards.
Don't pair high-saturation neon gradients behind body copy.
Don't use generic card grids repeating the same layout elements with generic icons.
Don't place tiny tracked uppercase text Kickers (eyebrows) above headings on every section.
